defs: A tuft, or other excrescence or natural ornament, growing on an animal's head; the comb of a cock; the swelling on the head of a serpent; the lengthened feathers of the crown or nape of bird, etc.
quotes: [Attack] his rising crest, and drive the serpent back.

defs: The plume of feathers, or other decoration, worn on a helmet; the distinctive ornament of a helmet, indicating the rank of the wearer; hence, also, the helmet.
quotes: And on his head there stood upright A crest, in token of a knight.

defs: A bearing worn, not upon the shield, but usually above it, or separately as an ornament for plate, liveries, and the like. It is a relic of the ancient cognizance. See Cognizance, 4.
The upper curve of a horse's neck.
quotes: Throwing the base thong from his bending crest.

defs: The ridge or top of a wave.
quotes: Like wave with crest of sparkling foam.

defs: The summit of a hill or mountain ridge.
The helm or head, as typical of a high spirit; pride; courage.
quotes: Now the time is come That France must vail her lofty plumed crest.

defs: The ornamental finishing which surmounts the ridge of a roof, canopy, etc.
quotes: The finials of gables and pinnacles are sometimes called crests.

defs: The top line of a slope or embankment.

records.morph: [imp. & p. p. Crested; p. pr. & vb. n. Cresting.]

defs: To furnish with, or surmount as, a crest; to serve as a crest for.
quotes: His legs bestrid the ocean, his reared arm Crested the world.
Mid groves of clouds that crest the mountain's brow.

defs: To mark with lines or streaks, like, or regarded as like, waving plumes.
quotes: Like as the shining sky in summer's night, . . . Is crested with lines of fiery light.
